text.title: api-gateway-observability Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for api-gateway-observability. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Configure per-route, per-consumer, and per-service metrics with consistent label sets: service, route, method, status_code, consumer. Aggregate at the dashboard; never store only aggregated metrics — label granularity enables root cause isolation.
rule.rationale: Without per-route metrics, a spike in errors on /api/payments is indistinguishable from a spike on /api/users in aggregated views. Label granularity is the difference between a 1-minute and 30-minute incident.

rule.statement: Log in JSON with these mandatory fields: timestamp (ISO8601), request_id/correlation_id, method, path, status, latency_ms, client_ip, user_agent, consumer (user ID or API key ID — never the raw key), upstream_latency_ms, response_size. Disable access logging for /health to avoid noise. Log at error level for 5xx with upstream error details.
rule.rationale: Without correlation_id, a client complaint about a failed request cannot be found in distributed logs. Without consumer identity, security incidents cannot be attributed. Without upstream_latency_ms, distinguishing gateway overhead from backend slowness is impossible.

rule.statement: Propagate W3C trace context (traceparent, tracestate) to all backend upstreams. Configure the gateway as the root span producer. Export traces via OTLP to a collector (Jaeger, Tempo, Zipkin). Sample at 10% by default; 100% for 5xx responses and requests exceeding p99 latency SLO.
rule.rationale: Without trace context propagation, distributed traces break at the gateway boundary — backend spans are orphaned. Without error-biased sampling, slow requests and failures are underrepresented in trace samples.
